summary: Loader stub pulls unrelated scoped dependency at require time under SQL/buffer cover content
details: Package advertises itself as a SQL access helper (name `@sql-access/nodesql`, install hint `npm i @sql-access/nods`) and the README is titled `bare-stream`, but index.js is a verbatim copy of Feross's `buffer` polyfill. The first executable line of index.js is `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');` — a top-level dynamic import of an unrelated scoped package declared as a runtime dependency in package.json (`"@sqlite-node/createsql": "^1.1.5"`). The imported value `ins` is never referenced anywhere else in the file, so the dynamic import has no functional role in the buffer implementation; it exists solely to load and execute the sibling package's code in the consumer's process whenever `@sql-access/nodesql` is required or imported. The three-way mismatch between package name (SQL), README (streaming), and code (buffer polyfill), combined with placeholder author metadata and an unused load-time fetch of an unrelated scoped package, matches the namespace-abuse loader-stub pattern: a benign-looking codebase used as cover for silently pulling attacker-controlled transitive code into the installer's tree.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
